verified.name: Humanoid

Website lists offices in London (HQ), Cambridge MA, and Burnaby BC.

changesProposed.name: ICON

current.name: ICON
current.founder: Jason Ballard, Evan Loomis, Alexander Le Roux

verified.notes: The sources provided describe a company called 'Icon' that is 'The Human Admaker' - a UGC (User Generated Content) advertising service founded by Kennan Frost, not the 3D-printed homes company referenced in the database entry. The database entry appears to refer to a different company (ICON, the construction/3D printing firm). Source [2] is a Wikipedia disambiguation page listing multiple entities named 'Icon' including 'ICON, a 3D concrete printing technology company' but provides no details. Source [3] is about a European humanoid robot startup and is not relevant to either Icon company. The sources do not contain information supporting the database entry's claims about 3D-printed homes, DoD/NASA contracts, or the stated founders (Jason Ballard, Evan Loomis, Alexander Le Roux).

verified.description: Designs and manufactures solar-powered, autonomous stratospheric aircraft operating above 60,000 feet for persistent surveillance and communications. Flagship platform APOLLO conducted flight testing in 2024.

Y Combinator Fall 2025 acceptance mentioned in database entry but not found in provided sources.

changesProposed.name: Reflect Orbital

verified.name: Reflect Orbital
verified.description: American space technology company that designs and builds satellites with large-scale deployable mirrors to redirect sunlight onto Earth's surface for responsive lighting and increased solar energy production.
verified.notes: Wikipedia states founding date as January 2021, but also mentions October 2021 in History section—sources show January 2021 as primary founding date. First satellite Eärendil-1 scheduled for mid-2026 launch. FCC granted license for first satellite on July 9, 2026 per Wikipedia. Total funding as of 2026 update: $35.2M (includes $6.5M seed round September 2024, $20M Series A May 2025, and $1.25M SBIR contract June 2025).

changesProposed.name: SpaceX

verified.name: SpaceX
verified.description: American spaceflight, telecommunications, and artificial intelligence company that conducts more orbital launches annually than any other launch provider and operates Starlink, a communications satellite constellation.
verified.notes: Wikipedia source indicates IPO occurred on June 12, 2026, raising $86B. Elon Musk owns 42% of outstanding shares and controls 85% of voting power (source notes 82% in one place, 85% in another—using 85% as stated in main text). Company has three operating divisions: Space, Connectivity (Starlink), and Artificial Intelligence (Grok, X, data centers). No valuation figure found in sources; database entry claims $1.5T IPO valuation but this is not supported by provided sources.
